_meta.description: HNP System in-app knowledge index. Used by the App Guide assistant to answer user questions about the application. Regenerate with: node scripts/buildAppKnowledge.js

entries.title: News Feed
entries.description: The News Feed shows company-wide announcements, updates, and notices posted for all staff.
entries.details: This page is accessible to all roles. It displays the latest company communications in reverse chronological order. Use it to stay up to date with business updates, policy changes, and internal announcements.

entries.title: Retail Dashboard
entries.description: The Retail Dashboard gives department managers a real-time overview of job card activity, staff, and performance.
entries.details: This page is available to Service Managers, Workshop Managers, and After Sales Directors. It aggregates job card counts, current workflow status, and team utilisation. Each role sees a dashboard tailored to their department's key metrics.

entries.title: Messages
entries.description: The Messages page provides internal team messaging between all staff members.
entries.details: All roles can access Messages. Use it to send direct messages to colleagues, discuss job cards, or coordinate across departments. Messages support threaded conversations. You can look up users by name or email to start a conversation.

entries.title: Job Cards
entries.description: The Job Cards page lists all active job cards across the workshop, with filters for status, priority, and department.
entries.details: Job Cards are the central record for any vehicle service or repair. Each card contains vehicle details, customer info, labour tasks, parts requests, notes, and status history. From this page you can create new job cards, search existing ones, filter by status or date, and open individual cards to view full details. Access is restricted to manager-level and above, plus Parts roles.

fields.name: Job Number
fields.description: Unique reference number assigned automatically when a job card is created.

fields.name: Status
fields.description: Current stage of the job: Pending, In Progress, Waiting Parts, Completed, or Invoiced.

fields.name: Priority
fields.description: Urgency level: Low, Normal, High, or Urgent. Used to sort the queue.

fields.name: Assigned To
fields.description: The technician or team member responsible for the job.

fields.name: Department
fields.description: The department handling the job, e.g. Workshop, Service, Parts.

fields.name: Booking Date
fields.description: When the customer booked the vehicle in.

fields.name: Start Date
fields.description: When work actually began on the vehicle.

fields.name: Estimated Cost
fields.description: Pre-work cost estimate given to the customer.

fields.name: Labour Cost
fields.description: Total cost of labour time recorded against the job.

fields.name: Parts Cost
fields.description: Total cost of parts ordered and used on the job.

entries.title: Next Jobs
entries.description: Next Jobs shows the prioritised queue of upcoming and waiting job cards ready to be assigned or started.
entries.details: This page is a filtered view of job cards that are booked or waiting to begin. Managers use it to plan the day's workload, assign technicians, and monitor queue depth. Jobs appear sorted by booking date and priority.

entries.title: My Jobs
entries.description: My Jobs shows each technician or MOT tester their own personally assigned job cards.
entries.details: Technicians and MOT Testers use this page as their personal work queue. Only jobs assigned to the logged-in user appear here. From this page they can view job details, log time entries, add notes, and update job status as work progresses.

entries.title: Archive Job
entries.description: The Archive page stores completed and closed job cards for historical reference.
entries.details: All roles can access the Archive page. It shows job cards that have been marked as closed or archived. Use it to look up past jobs, check historical costs, or reference previous work on a vehicle. Archived jobs cannot be edited.

entries.title: Tracker
entries.description: The Tracker page shows a live board of vehicle and job progress across the workshop.
entries.details: The Tracker provides a visual overview of where vehicles are in the service process. Staff can see which jobs are active, which are waiting, and where each vehicle is located within the workshop workflow. It is used by Techs, Service, Managers, Valet Service, and Admins. This is the same section referenced by the /tracking slash command in Floating Notes.

entries.title: Clocking
entries.description: The Clocking page lets Workshop Managers view and manage staff clock-in and clock-out records.
entries.details: Workshop Managers use this page to monitor technician attendance and time worked. It shows who is clocked in, their start times, and daily hour totals. This is separate from time entries on job cards, which track labour against specific jobs. Access is restricted to Workshop Managers.

entries.title: Valet Jobs
entries.description: The Valet Jobs page shows the current queue of vehicles assigned to the valet service team.
entries.details: Valet Service staff use this page to see which vehicles need cleaning and detailing before handover. Jobs appear here when a job card reaches the valet stage of the workflow. Staff can update the status of valet work from this page.

entries.title: Stock Catalogue
entries.description: The Stock Catalogue lists all parts and inventory items held in the workshop stock system.
entries.details: Parts staff and Parts Managers use this page to browse, search, and manage the stock of spare parts. Each item shows quantity on hand, part number, description, and location. Use it to check availability before ordering or to update stock levels after receiving goods.

entries.title: Goods In
entries.description: Goods In is where incoming parts deliveries are received and matched against parts requests.
entries.details: When parts ordered for job cards arrive, staff use this page to record receipt and match items to their corresponding parts requests. This updates stock levels and notifies the relevant job card that parts are available. Multiple roles use Goods In as part of the parts ordering workflow.

entries.title: Parts Deliveries
entries.description: Parts Deliveries tracks outstanding and completed parts orders placed for job cards.
entries.details: This page is used by Parts and Parts Managers to monitor what has been ordered, from which suppliers, and when it is expected to arrive. It gives visibility over the full parts ordering pipeline from request to delivery.

entries.title: Parts Manager Dashboard
entries.description: The Parts Manager Dashboard gives the Parts Manager an overview of stock, orders, and parts activity.
entries.details: This dedicated dashboard is only available to Parts Managers. It summarises stock levels, pending orders, goods received, and parts request volumes. Use it to manage the parts department and spot stock shortfalls or ordering patterns.

entries.title: Tech Efficiency
entries.description: Tech Efficiency shows each technician or MOT tester their own performance and labour efficiency metrics.
entries.details: Technicians and MOT Testers can view their job completion rates, hours worked, labour efficiency ratios, and other performance indicators on this page. It is a personal view — each user sees only their own data.

entries.title: Request Consumables
entries.description: Technicians use this page to request workshop consumables such as oils, filters, and materials.
entries.details: Techs can submit requests for consumable items needed to complete their jobs. Requests are sent to the Workshop Manager for approval and fulfilment. This keeps consumable usage tracked and prevents uncontrolled stock depletion.

entries.title: Consumables Tracker
entries.description: The Consumables Tracker is the Workshop Manager's workspace for managing and fulfilling consumable requests.
entries.details: Workshop Managers use this page to see all pending consumable requests from Techs, approve or decline them, and manage stock levels for workshop supplies. It is the counterpart to the Tech's Consumables Request page.

entries.title: HR Manager
entries.description: The HR Manager page is the Owner's workspace for managing staff records, HR administration, and payroll-related tasks.
entries.details: This section is restricted to Owners only. It provides tools for managing employee records, reviewing staff information, and handling HR-sensitive data. It is not accessible to any other role including Admin Managers.

entries.title: User Admin
entries.description: User Admin is where Admin Managers and Owners create, edit, and manage user accounts and their roles.
entries.details: From this page you can add new staff members, update their roles and access levels, reset passwords, and deactivate accounts. Role assignments made here directly control what each user can see and do across the entire system. Only Admin Managers and Owners can access this page.

fields.name: Role
fields.description: The user's job role, which controls their access across the system.

fields.name: Email
fields.description: The user's email address used for login.

fields.name: First Name / Last Name
fields.description: The user's display name shown across the app.

entries.title: Profile
entries.description: The Profile page shows the logged-in user's own account details and personal preferences.
entries.details: All users can access their own Profile. It shows their name, role, email, and any personal settings. Users can update their profile details from here. This is also where personal dashboard settings and preferences may be stored.

entries.title: Job Card System
entries.description: Job Cards are the central workflow unit of the system — every vehicle service or repair is tracked through a job card.
entries.details: A Job Card is created for every vehicle brought in for service or repair. It captures the vehicle, customer, labour tasks, parts, notes, time entries, costs, and status at every stage. Job Cards flow through status stages from Pending → In Progress → Completed → Invoiced. Multiple departments interact with the same job card: Service creates it, Techs work on it, Parts supplies components, and Managers oversee it. The job number is the primary reference used across the app.

entries.title: Parts Requests
entries.description: Parts Requests allow staff to order parts required for specific job cards, tracking the full order-to-receipt lifecycle.
entries.details: When a job requires parts, a Parts Request is created against the job card. The request specifies the part number, description, quantity, and supplier. Parts staff manage the request through ordering, awaiting delivery, and goods-in receipt. Once received, the job card is updated and work can continue. The status of each parts request is visible from the job card and from the Parts section of the app.

entries.title: Job Notes
entries.description: Job Notes are text notes attached directly to a specific job card, visible to all staff working on that job.
entries.details: Job Notes are different from Floating Notes. They are attached to a specific job card and persist as part of that job's history. Notes can be marked as internal (hidden from customer) or visible in customer-facing documents. Multiple notes can be added to a single job card by different team members. Notes appear in the job card's notes tab with timestamps and author names.

entries.title: Floating Notes
entries.description: Floating Notes is the persistent notes widget accessible from any page via the draggable bubble in the corner of the screen.
entries.details: The Floating Notes widget appears as a small draggable button on every page. Click it to open the notes panel. You can create multiple note tabs, each with a title and rich text description. Notes can be personal (only you see them) or Global (visible to all users). You can also Share a note with specific users. The panel can be resized and repositioned. Notes are automatically saved as you type. Use the / commands in the description to link to jobs, customers, vehicles, or navigate to sections of the app.

entries.title: Internal Messaging
entries.description: The messaging system lets all staff send and receive internal messages with any colleague.
entries.details: The messaging system supports thread-based conversations between any users in the system. Search for a colleague by name or email to start a conversation. Messages are not role-restricted — all staff can message each other regardless of department. This is different from Floating Notes, which is a personal scratchpad, not a communication tool.

entries.title: Time Entries
entries.description: Time Entries track how much time technicians spend on each job card, used for calculating labour costs.
entries.details: Technicians can clock on and off individual job cards using Time Entries. Each entry records the start time, end time, and duration. Time entries roll up into the Labour Cost displayed on the job card. This is separate from the Clocking page, which tracks attendance rather than job-specific time.

entries.title: MOT Tests
entries.description: MOT Test records are stored against vehicles and tracked through the job card system.
entries.details: MOT Testers manage MOT test records through their assigned job cards. Test results, advisory items, and fail reasons are recorded against the vehicle's history. MOT Testers access their workload through My Jobs. Test history is visible on the vehicle record.

entries.title: Customer Management
entries.description: Customer records store contact details, vehicle history, and job card history for each customer.
entries.details: Each customer in the system has a profile with their name, email, phone, and address. Customer records link to all vehicles they own and all job cards associated with those vehicles. You can use the /cust or /addcust slash commands in Floating Notes to quickly reference or create customer records.

entries.title: Vehicle Management
entries.description: Vehicle records store registration details, specifications, service history, and MOT history.
entries.details: Each vehicle in the system has a record with registration plate, make, model, year, VIN, colour, mileage, fuel type, engine size, and transmission. Vehicles link to their owner (customer), all job cards, MOT tests, and sales. Vehicle records are created when a job card is raised for an unknown vehicle.

entries.title: Role-Based Access Control
entries.description: Role-Based Access Control determines which pages and features each user can see and use based on their assigned role.
entries.details: Every user is assigned a role in User Admin. That role controls which sidebar menu items appear, which pages they can navigate to, and which actions they can perform. Roles are defined in the navigation config (src/config/navigation.js) with arrays of allowed roles per section. The role guard (src/lib/auth/roleGuard.js) enforces this on all API endpoints. Roles are managed exclusively by Admin Managers and Owners through the User Admin page.

entries.title: What is a Job Card?
entries.description: A Job Card is the primary record for every vehicle service or repair carried out at the workshop.
entries.details: A Job Card brings together all information about a single service job: the customer, vehicle, work description, assigned technician, parts used, time spent, costs, and final status. It acts as a work order, a costing document, and a historical record. Every job in the system has a unique job number for reference. Job cards pass through status stages from Pending through to Invoiced. Multiple departments interact with the same job card throughout its lifecycle.

entries.title: What is Page Access?
entries.description: Page Access refers to which roles are permitted to view a specific page or use a specific feature in the system.
entries.details: Each page and section of the system has a list of allowed roles. If your role is not in that list, the page will not appear in your sidebar and you cannot navigate to it directly. For example, the HR Manager page is only accessible to Owners. The Job Cards page is visible to Admin Managers, Service Managers, Workshop Managers, Aftersales Managers, Parts, and Parts Managers but not to Technicians or Valet Service staff. Access is managed through User Admin by assigning the correct role to each user. If you believe you should have access to a page but cannot see it, ask an Admin Manager or Owner to check your role.

entries.title: What is a Global Note?
entries.description: A Global Note is a floating note that is visible to all users in the system, not just the creator.
entries.details: When you create a note in the Floating Notes widget, it is private by default — only you can see it. By toggling the ON/OFF button on the right side of the Title row, you make the note Global, meaning every user in the system can see it in their own Notes panel. Global notes are useful for team-wide reminders, announcements, or reference information. You cannot edit a global note created by another user — it will appear as read-only. To remove a global note from shared visibility, toggle it back to OFF.

entries.title: Note Sharing
entries.description: Note Sharing lets you share a specific Floating Note with selected colleagues without making it visible to everyone.
entries.details: The Share button in the Title row of the Floating Notes panel opens a user picker. You can search for colleagues by name or email and tick the ones you want to share with. Shared notes appear in the selected users' Notes panels as read-only. Unlike Global notes (which go to everyone), Share gives you precise control over who sees your note. You must be the owner of a note to share it. Notes shared with you appear alongside your own notes but are read-only.

entries.title: Job Card Statuses
entries.description: Job cards move through a series of statuses that reflect the current stage of the work.
entries.details: The main job card statuses are: Pending (booked but not started), In Progress (work underway), Waiting Parts (work paused waiting for parts), Completed (work finished), and Invoiced (customer has been billed). Some workflows may include additional intermediate states. Status changes are logged automatically in the status history. Managers can filter job cards by status on the Job Cards page.

entries.title: Status History
entries.description: Status History is a log of every status change a job card has gone through, with timestamps and the user who made the change.
entries.details: Status History records every time a job card's status field was changed. For example: who moved it from Pending to In Progress, and when. It is an audit trail for the lifecycle of the job's official status. This is different from Workflow History, which tracks a broader range of changes to the job record.

entries.title: Workflow History
entries.description: Workflow History is the full audit log of all meaningful changes to a job card, not just status changes.
entries.details: Workflow History captures a broader set of events than Status History. It records changes to fields like assignee, priority, notes additions, parts request updates, cost changes, and more. Think of Status History as the headline summary ('job moved to Completed') and Workflow History as the detailed timeline of everything that happened to the job card. Both are accessible from within the job card view.

entries.title: Slash Commands in Floating Notes
entries.description: Slash commands are keyboard shortcuts in the Floating Notes description field that let you quickly link to app sections or records.
entries.details: While typing in the description of a Floating Note, type / to see a list of available commands. Available commands include: /job[number] to reference a job card, /cust[name] to reference a customer, /vehicle to reference a vehicle, /parts for the parts section, /tracking for the tracker, /valet for valet, /hr for HR, /clocking for clocking, /myjobs for my jobs, /invoice[number] for an invoice, /order[ordernumber] for a parts order, and others. Press Tab or Enter to apply a command. The command will be inserted as a reference in your note text. These are for personal note organisation and navigation shortcuts.

entries.title: Admin Manager Role
entries.description: Admin Managers have broad administrative access across job cards, user management, and department operations.
entries.details: Admin Managers can access: Next Jobs, Job Cards, and User Admin. They are classed as a core HR and admin role. They can create and manage user accounts and assign roles. They can view the full job card list and manage department operations. They cannot access the HR Manager page (that is Owner-only).

entries.title: Owner Role
entries.description: The Owner role has the highest level of access in the system, including HR Manager and User Admin.
entries.details: Owners can access all the same areas as Admin Managers, plus the HR Manager page. The Owner role is typically reserved for the business owner or director. They can manage all user accounts and roles, and access HR-sensitive staff information. In the role hierarchy, Owner sits above Admin Manager for HR-related access.

entries.title: Service Manager Role
entries.description: Service Managers oversee service department operations including job card management and parts goods-in.
entries.details: Service Managers can access: Retail Dashboard, Next Jobs, Job Cards, and Goods In. They can see the full job card list for their department, manage the incoming job queue, and process parts deliveries. They cannot access HR or User Admin.

entries.title: Workshop Manager Role
entries.description: Workshop Managers run the workshop floor, managing job cards, clocking, consumables, and goods-in.
entries.details: Workshop Managers can access: Retail Dashboard, Next Jobs, Job Cards, Clocking, Consumables Tracker, and Goods In. They manage technician attendance through Clocking, oversee consumable usage, and handle parts receipts. They can see all job cards across the workshop.

entries.title: Aftersales Manager Role
entries.description: Aftersales Managers handle post-sale service and can access job cards, next jobs, and goods-in.
entries.details: Aftersales Managers can access: Next Jobs, Job Cards, and Goods In. They deal with the post-sale service side of the business, following up on customer vehicles and managing aftercare job cards.

entries.title: Technician (Techs) Role
entries.description: Technicians work on their assigned job cards, track their time, and request consumables.
entries.details: Technicians can access: Tracker, Archive Job, My Jobs, Consumables Request, and Tech Efficiency. They see only their own assigned jobs through My Jobs. They cannot see the full Job Cards list or manage other users' work. Techs clock time against individual job cards. They request consumables through the dedicated request page.

entries.title: MOT Tester Role
entries.description: MOT Testers manage their assigned MOT test jobs and track their efficiency.
entries.details: MOT Testers can access: Tracker, Archive Job, My Jobs, and Tech Efficiency. They are similar to Technicians in access level. MOT Tests are conducted through job cards assigned specifically to them. They see only their own assigned jobs.

entries.title: Parts Role
entries.description: Parts staff manage stock, goods-in, deliveries, and the stock catalogue.
entries.details: Parts staff can access: Job Cards, Stock Catalogue, Goods In, and Deliveries. They handle the day-to-day management of parts stock, receiving deliveries, and processing parts requests from job cards. They cannot access HR, clocking, or user management.

entries.title: Parts Manager Role
entries.description: Parts Managers have all Parts access plus a dedicated dashboard and manager-level controls.
entries.details: Parts Managers can access everything the Parts role can, plus the dedicated Parts Manager Dashboard. They have oversight of the entire parts operation including ordering, stock management, and supplier relationships.

entries.title: Valet Service Role
entries.description: Valet Service staff manage their vehicle preparation and cleaning jobs.
entries.details: Valet Service staff can access: Tracker and Valet Jobs. They see vehicles assigned to them for cleaning and preparation before customer handover. Their access is limited to the valet workflow — they cannot access job cards or management sections.

entries.title: Service Role
entries.description: Service staff handle customer-facing service desk operations including job cards and parts goods-in.
entries.details: Service staff can access: Tracker, Archive Job, Job Cards, and Goods In. They work at the service desk, booking vehicles in, managing job cards, and processing parts deliveries. They have similar job card access to managers but without the management-level reporting tools.

entries.title: How to Create a Job Card
entries.description: Step-by-step guide to creating a new job card for a vehicle service or repair.
entries.details: Creating a job card records the customer, vehicle, and work required in one place.
steps: Navigate to Job Cards from the sidebar.
Click the 'New Job Card' or 'Create' button at the top of the page.
Select or create the customer — search by name, email, or phone.
Select or create the vehicle — search by registration plate or VIN.
Enter the work description detailing what needs to be done.
Set the Priority (Low, Normal, High, Urgent) and Booking Date.
Assign the job to a technician if known at this stage.
Add any initial notes in the Notes tab.
Save the job card. A job number is assigned automatically.
The job card is now in Pending status and visible in Next Jobs.

entries.title: How to Add a Note to a Job Card
entries.description: How to add an internal or customer-facing note to a specific job card.
steps: Open the job card you want to add a note to.
Click the 'Notes' tab inside the job card view.
Type your note text in the input field.
If this note should be hidden from the customer, check 'Internal Only'.
Click 'Add Note' or press Enter to save.
The note appears immediately with your name and a timestamp.
Other users viewing the same job card can see the note.

entries.title: How to Share a Floating Note
entries.description: How to share one of your personal Floating Notes with specific colleagues.
steps: Open the Floating Notes panel by clicking the N bubble on the screen.
Select the note tab you want to share.
Make sure you are the owner of the note (you must have created it — shared or global notes from others cannot be reshared).
Click the 'Share' button on the right side of the Title row.
A user picker opens. Search by name or email.
Tick the checkboxes next to the colleagues you want to share with.
Changes are saved automatically — no confirm button needed.
The note will appear in the selected users' Notes panels as read-only.

entries.title: How to Make a Note Global
entries.description: How to toggle a Floating Note to be visible to all users.
steps: Open the Floating Notes panel.
Select the note you want to make global.
You must be the owner of the note.
Find the ON/OFF toggle button on the right side of the Title row.
Click it to toggle ON. The button turns highlighted when ON.
The note is now Global — all users will see it in their Notes panels.
To remove global visibility, click the toggle again to set it back to OFF.

entries.title: How to Request Parts for a Job
entries.description: How to raise a parts request against a job card so that needed parts can be ordered.
steps: Open the relevant job card.
Click the 'Parts' or 'Parts Requests' tab inside the job card.
Click 'Add Part' or 'Request Part'.
Enter the part number, description, and quantity required.
Select the supplier if known.
Save the parts request. It will appear in the parts request queue.
Parts staff will see the request and process the order.
When the parts arrive, they are received through Goods In.
The job card status updates to show parts have been received.
Work can continue once parts are received.

entries.title: How to Clock Time on a Job
entries.description: How technicians clock their time against a specific job card.
steps: Navigate to My Jobs in the sidebar.
Find the job you are starting work on.
Click into the job card.
Use the time tracking / clock-in control to start timing.
Work on the job. The timer runs in the background.
When you finish or pause work, use the clock-out control to stop the timer.
The time entry is saved against the job card automatically.
Your recorded hours will appear in the Labour section of the job card.

entries.title: How to Archive a Job Card
entries.description: How to archive a completed job card to move it out of the active job list.
steps: Open the completed job card.
Ensure the status has been set to Completed or Invoiced.
Look for the Archive option — usually a button or action menu item.
Confirm the archive action.
The job card moves out of the active list and into the Archive section.
Archived jobs can still be viewed at /job-cards/archive but cannot be edited.

entries.title: How to Find a Customer
entries.description: How to search for an existing customer record in the system.
steps: From any Job Card creation or edit form, use the customer search field.
Type the customer's name, email, or phone number.
Matching records appear in the dropdown.
Select the correct customer to link them to the job.
Alternatively, use the /cust[name] slash command in Floating Notes to reference a customer quickly.

entries.title: How to Add a New User
entries.description: How to create a new user account and assign a role for a new staff member.
steps: Navigate to User Admin in the sidebar (requires Admin Manager or Owner role).
Click 'Add User' or 'New User'.
Enter the staff member's first name, last name, and email address.
Set a temporary password (they can change it on first login).
Assign the appropriate role from the role dropdown.
Save the user account.
The user can now log in with their email and the password you set.
Their role controls which pages and features are visible to them immediately.

entries.title: How to Use Slash Commands in Floating Notes
entries.description: How to use the / shortcut system in the Floating Notes description field.
steps: Open the Floating Notes panel.
Click into the Description field of any note.
Type a forward slash /
A dropdown appears showing available commands.
Keep typing to filter the list, e.g. /job to see job-related commands.
Use arrow keys to navigate and Tab or Enter to apply a command.
The command is inserted as a reference or link in your note text.
You can use /job123 to reference job card 123, /custSmith to reference a Smith customer, etc.

entries.title: Where to Configure Floating Notes Permissions
entries.description: Floating Notes is available to all authenticated users. Access is controlled by authentication rather than role, but sharing and global visibility have their own controls.
entries.details: Floating Notes is accessible to any logged-in user — there is no role restriction on the widget itself. However, within the Notes panel: the Share button is only enabled if you own the note, the Global toggle is only enabled for note owners, and deleting a tab is restricted to the note owner. The widget code is in src/components/GlobalNotesWidget.js. The database layer is in src/lib/database/floatingNotes.js. If you want to restrict who can create or view global notes at an infrastructure level, that would require modifying the Supabase RLS policies on the floating_notes table, or adding a role check to the floatingNotes database functions.
